mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate accelerates rapidly, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity valuations over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production declines while consumer credit simultaneously expands, it indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand is being met by inventory drawdowns rather than sustainable growth, forecasting negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a sustained increase in industrial production reflects capacity restoration that boosts corporate profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a full quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns in confirmed recovery episodes do not exceed the historical median by at least 0.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential rise in real GDP growth compresses equity risk premiums by confirming economic resilience, leading to systematically positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 12-month rolling window, or forward returns in high-GDP-growth quarters are not statistically distinct from flat-growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, borrowing costs suppress corporate investment and consumer spending, triggering a contraction in expected earnings that drives negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive in more than 65% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the threshold signal shows no incremental predictive power over a linear FEDFUNDS specification.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment growth decelerates while housing starts remain elevated, it signals a labor market cooling that will eventually dampen consumption-driven corporate revenues, forecasting negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to significantly predict negative returns in walk-forward tests, or the combined signal's hit rate drops below 45% over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansion environments, a sustained decline in housing starts acts as a leading indicator for broader demand contraction, systematically depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Forward returns in declining housing periods do not significantly underperform rising housing periods, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% in out-of-sample testing.
